steps.title: Map the opening question
steps.content: The text begins with an unresolved scientific question. Human activities influence carbon and nitrogen in soil, but scientists are unsure how deeply those effects extend. Eze and colleagues then propose a hypothesis about that question: differences in land use may affect carbon and nitrogen levels below the topsoil layer, not just from 0 to 30 cm.

steps.title: Use the conflicting result
steps.content: The final sentence shifts with though, showing that the study result did not support the hypothesis. Beyond 30 cm, carbon and nitrogen dropped to comparably low levels across land-use types, so the expected land-use differences did not continue below the topsoil.

steps.title: Eliminate mismatched structures
steps.content: Choice A adds refinement that the text never describes. Choice C invents competing hypotheses and a conclusion that one was likely correct. Choice D says the data validate the hypothesis, but the result conflicts with it. The structure is unresolved question, hypothesis, then conflicting observation. The answer is B.
